  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Copper tubing cut ends were not reamed, leaving a visible internal burr."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the affected joints, ream the copper tubing to its full inside diameter, and re-solder or re-press the joints."
  ],
  "rationale": "Leaving copper tubing unreamed restricts flow, causes localized turbulence, and can lead to premature erosion-corrosion failure of the copper piping system. Both the International Plumbing Code (IPC) and manufacturer specifications require all cut pipe and tubing to be reamed to the full inside diameter of the tube to remove burrs.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 605.14.1",
    "Copper Development Association (CDA) Copper Tube Handbook"
  ]
}
